title: Held Light
description: B minor, 134 BPM, Bm to G, 2-step drums, and both a stab and a lead. The instructions are exact enough for a beginner, but the final result is a fuller 2-step song with real development between drops.
what_youll_learn: How to write a 2-step groove that still feels steady and heavy
How i to bVI works in B minor using alternating 1-bar harmony
How to separate a stab layer from a lead layer in a denser arrangement
How to use section-by-section level and send changes to make drop 2 hit harder

steps.title: Set up the project
steps.instruction: Create a new Ableton Live set at 134 BPM and save it as 'original-10-held-light'. Make these tracks in order: DRUMS, BASS, PAD, STAB, LEAD, and a return track named VERB. The whole song lives on one two-chord motion: B minor to G major, but the rhythm section is now 2-step instead of 4-on-floor.
steps.why: Changing only the groove while keeping harmony simple lets you hear exactly what 2-step does to the feel of a garage song.
steps.tip: This track should feel less march-like than the 4-on-floor songs and more like it is sliding around the beat.

steps.title: Load the 2-step drum kit
steps.instruction: Load a Drum Rack on DRUMS. Put these exact samples on these exact pads: C1 = 'Production Hut- UK Garage Drums Volume 3/DRUMS ONE SHOTS/KICK/PH_UKG3_KICK_05.wav', D1 = 'Production Hut- UK Garage Drums Volume 2/One Shots/Clap/PH_UKG2_Clap_24.wav', F#1 = 'Production Hut- UK Garage Drums Volume 3/DRUMS ONE SHOTS/CLOSED/PH_UKG3_CLOSED_8.wav', G#1 = 'Production Hut- UK Garage Drums Volume 3/DRUMS ONE SHOTS/SHAKER/PH_UKG3_SHAKER_01.wav'.
steps.why: The tighter Volume 3 kick and closed hat work well for 2-step because the groove relies more on gaps and syncopation than on constant pulse.
steps.tip: The clap is still the anchor on beats 2 and 4 even though the kick stops being constant.

steps.title: Program the 2-step pattern
steps.instruction: Create a 2-bar DRUMS clip. All note lengths are 0.25 beats. KICK C1: 1.1.1 vel 97, 1.2.3 vel 84, 1.4.4 vel 76, 2.1.3 vel 88, 2.3.1 vel 82, 2.4.3 vel 78. CLAP D1: 1.2.1 vel 92, 1.4.1 vel 90, 2.2.1 vel 92, 2.4.1 vel 90. CLOSED HAT F#1: 1.1.3 vel 64, 1.2.4 vel 58, 1.3.3 vel 63, 1.4.3 vel 60, 2.1.3 vel 64, 2.2.4 vel 58, 2.3.3 vel 64, 2.4.3 vel 60. SHAKER G#1: 1.1.4 vel 42, 1.3.4 vel 40, 2.1.4 vel 42, 2.3.4 vel 40. Group DRUMS and add Glue Compressor with Ratio 4:1, Attack 3 ms, Release Auto, Threshold -5 dB, Soft Clip On.
steps.why: The kick pattern creates the broken, skippy motion that makes 2-step feel like garage instead of house. The clap keeps the listener oriented.
steps.tip: Do not quantize these notes to a different pattern after drawing them. The exact gaps are what make the groove work.

steps.title: Write the bass pattern
steps.instruction: On BASS load Serum 2 and open S1 Presets -> Garage -> 'Bass- Knocking Sub'. Leave the preset exactly as loaded. Create a 4-bar clip with these notes: B1 at 1.1.1, length 1.5 beats, velocity 92; F#1 at 1.2.3, length 0.5 beats, velocity 74; B1 at 1.3.1, length 1.0 beats, velocity 90; D2 at 1.4.3, length 0.5 beats, velocity 72; G1 at 2.1.1, length 1.5 beats, velocity 89; D2 at 2.2.3, length 0.5 beats, velocity 72; G1 at 2.3.1, length 1.0 beats, velocity 87; B1 at 2.4.3, length 0.5 beats, velocity 70; B1 at 3.1.1, length 1.5 beats, velocity 92; F#1 at 3.2.3, length 0.5 beats, velocity 74; B1 at 3.3.1, length 1.0 beats, velocity 90; D2 at 3.4.3, length 0.5 beats, velocity 72; G1 at 4.1.1, length 2.5 beats, velocity 89; D2 at 4.3.3, length 0.5 beats, velocity 70; A1 at 4.4.3, length 0.5 beats, velocity 66.
steps.why: Alternating 1-bar Bm and G harmonies makes the track feel more restless than the longer held-chord songs, which fits the 2-step groove.
steps.tip: The A1 at the end is not part of the chord. It is the exact leading note that throws you back into the next B1.

steps.title: Process the bass
steps.instruction: After Serum add Saturator, Compressor, EQ Eight, and Utility. Saturator: Drive 4.6 dB, Soft Clip On, Output -2.8 dB. Compressor: Sidechain On, Audio From DRUMS, Threshold -18 dB, Ratio 4:1, Attack 0.30 ms, Release 80 ms. EQ Eight: High-Pass 30 Hz at 24 dB slope. Utility: Width 0 percent. Set BASS fader to -5.0 dB.
steps.why: The bass release is short because the kick pattern is more broken. The low end has to recover quickly between hits.
steps.tip: If the groove feels stiff, the answer is almost never to lengthen the bass notes in this song.

steps.title: Load the pad and lock the voicings
steps.instruction: On PAD load Serum 2 and open S1 Presets -> Garage -> 'Pad- Doubt'. Leave the preset exactly as loaded. Your chords are B minor = B, D, F# and G major = G, B, D.
steps.why: B and D are shared between the two chords, which is why the move sounds luminous instead of dramatic.
steps.tip: This track is about subtle light, not a huge harmonic surprise.

steps.title: Program the pad, return reverb, and sidechain
steps.instruction: Create a 4-bar PAD clip. Draw D4 at 1.1.1, length 3.5 beats, velocity 72; F#4 at 1.1.1, length 3.5 beats, velocity 68; B4 at 1.1.1, length 3.5 beats, velocity 64; B3 at 2.1.1, length 3.5 beats, velocity 72; D4 at 2.1.1, length 3.5 beats, velocity 68; G4 at 2.1.1, length 3.5 beats, velocity 64; D4 at 3.1.1, length 3.5 beats, velocity 72; F#4 at 3.1.1, length 3.5 beats, velocity 68; B4 at 3.1.1, length 3.5 beats, velocity 64; B3 at 4.1.1, length 3.5 beats, velocity 72; D4 at 4.1.1, length 3.5 beats, velocity 68; G4 at 4.1.1, length 3.5 beats, velocity 64. On VERB add Reverb with Decay Time 2.5 s, Pre-Delay 10 ms, Dry/Wet 100 percent, then EQ Eight High-Pass 220 Hz. On PAD add Compressor with Sidechain On, Audio From DRUMS, Threshold -22 dB, Ratio 4:1, Attack 0.10 ms, Release 125 ms. Set PAD send to VERB at -18.0 dB and PAD fader to -11.5 dB.
steps.why: Using upper inversions keeps the pad from crowding the sub bass while still giving the harmony enough body to feel rich.
steps.tip: Keep the pad high enough in register that it feels like light over the bass rather than a second bass instrument.

steps.title: Add the stab rhythm
steps.instruction: On STAB load Serum 2 and open S1 Presets -> Garage -> 'Stab - Eza'. Leave the preset exactly as loaded. Create a 4-bar clip with these notes: F#4 at 1.1.3, length 0.5 beats, velocity 82; D4 at 1.3.3, length 0.25 beats, velocity 74; B4 at 1.4.3, length 0.25 beats, velocity 78; D4 at 2.1.3, length 0.5 beats, velocity 80; G4 at 2.3.3, length 0.25 beats, velocity 74; B4 at 2.4.3, length 0.25 beats, velocity 76; F#4 at 3.1.3, length 0.5 beats, velocity 82; D4 at 3.3.3, length 0.25 beats, velocity 74; D4 at 4.1.3, length 0.5 beats, velocity 80; G4 at 4.3.3, length 0.25 beats, velocity 74. Set STAB send to VERB at -20.0 dB and STAB fader to -14.0 dB.
steps.why: The stab sits in the pockets between the 2-step kicks, so it adds motion without flattening the groove.
steps.tip: This part should sound like flashes between the kicks, not like it is trying to sit on top of them.

steps.title: Add the lead line
steps.instruction: On LEAD load Serum 2 and open S1 Presets -> Garage -> 'Lead- Saturn'. Leave the preset exactly as loaded. Create a 4-bar clip with these notes: B4 at 1.1.1, length 1.0 beats, velocity 82; F#4 at 1.2.3, length 0.5 beats, velocity 76; D4 at 1.4.1, length 0.5 beats, velocity 72; G4 at 2.1.1, length 1.0 beats, velocity 80; D4 at 2.3.1, length 0.5 beats, velocity 74; B3 at 2.4.1, length 0.5 beats, velocity 72; B4 at 3.1.1, length 1.0 beats, velocity 82; D5 at 3.2.3, length 0.5 beats, velocity 76; F#4 at 3.4.1, length 0.5 beats, velocity 74; G4 at 4.1.1, length 1.5 beats, velocity 81; D4 at 4.3.1, length 0.5 beats, velocity 74; B3 at 4.4.1, length 0.5 beats, velocity 72. After Serum add Simple Delay with Time Left 1/8, Time Right 1/8, Feedback 14 percent, Dry/Wet 10 percent. Set LEAD send to VERB at -19.0 dB and LEAD fader to -10.0 dB.
steps.why: The lead is longer and smoother than the stab, so the two layers feel like different instruments instead of duplicates with different sounds.
steps.tip: In the first drop the stab should feel more important. In the second drop the lead should claim more attention.

steps.title: Create the vocal pocket
steps.instruction: On PAD add EQ Eight after the Compressor: High-Pass 140 Hz, Bell 900 Hz at -3.5 dB with Q 1.50, Bell 2.5 kHz at -2.5 dB with Q 1.30. On STAB add EQ Eight: High-Pass 260 Hz, Bell 2.8 kHz at -2.0 dB with Q 1.30. On LEAD add EQ Eight after Simple Delay: High-Pass 220 Hz, Bell 3.0 kHz at -2.0 dB with Q 1.20.
steps.why: With two melodic layers, this step is what stops the middle of the mix from turning into a solid block.
steps.tip: If the stab and lead still blur together, lower the STAB fader first. The lead is the more emotional part.

steps.title: Arrange the full track with a bigger second drop
steps.instruction: Build the arrangement exactly like this. Bars 1-8: intro, DRUMS only, and automate the DRUMS group Utility Gain from -2 dB at bar 1 to 0 dB at bar 8. Bars 9-24: drop 1, DRUMS plus BASS plus PAD plus STAB. Bars 25-32: break, PAD plus STAB only for bars 25-28, then PAD only for bars 29-32 with PAD send to VERB moving from -18 dB to -14 dB. Delete all clips on bar 32 beat 4 for a 1-beat silence. Bars 33-48: drop 2A, full arrangement except LEAD. Bars 49-64: drop 2B, turn LEAD on and raise STAB fader by +1 dB only from bars 57-64. Bars 65-72: outro, mute LEAD at bar 65, mute STAB at bar 67, mute PAD at bar 69, mute BASS at bar 71, let DRUMS finish bar 72.
steps.why: The song gets bigger in stages: first groove, then stab, then lead, then a slight stab lift at the tail. That staggered growth is how fuller arrangements stay readable.
steps.tip: If bars 49-64 feel too crowded, the fix is usually a slightly lower STAB, not a lower LEAD.

steps.title: Set the mix
steps.instruction: Set DRUMS group to -4.0 dB, BASS to -5.0 dB, PAD to -11.5 dB, STAB to -14.0 dB, LEAD to -10.0 dB, VERB return to -18.5 dB, and Master to 0.0 dB. Add Limiter on Master with Ceiling -0.30 dB.
steps.why: The 2-step kick pattern needs space to feel punchy, so the upper layers stay slightly lower than you might expect when soloed.
steps.tip: Judge the mix from bars 49-64. That is the densest section and therefore the best reality check.

steps.title: Export Held Light
steps.instruction: Export bars 1 through 72 as WAV, 24-bit, 44.1 kHz with filename 'original-10-held-light-v1.wav'. After export, compare bars 9-24 and 49-64 and confirm the second drop feels richer because of the added lead and automation, not because the whole track simply got louder.
steps.why: That comparison is the core lesson: arrangement density and exact automation can create lift without sacrificing clarity.
steps.tip: If the second drop only feels louder, back off the STAB boost in bars 57-64 before changing anything else.
